skill1.kitLine: Magnum Ammo: Normal Attack Damage Multiplier ▲ 30% for 9 round(s) (on battle start / reload to max ammo)
skill1.note: 30% value correct. 'For 9 rounds' duration and reload-refresh trigger collapsed to permanent passive — justified by rolling reload (reload_start_ammo=8, reloadFrames 0) so she effectively always has max ammo. charFixes.reloadFrames=0 supports this.

skill1.kitLine: True Damage ▲ 34.99% for 10 sec (on burst cast)
skill1.note: Block present with correct value/duration. HOWEVER ks-extracts finding F1: burst true-dmg conversion is unmodeled in the engine (needs weaponSwap trueNormals), making trueDamagePct 34.99 ENGINE-INERT. The block exists but likely contributes zero damage.

skill2.kitLine: Acid Ammo: first round deals 192% of final ATK as sustained damage every 1 sec for 30 sec (on battle start / reload to max ammo)
skill2.where: skill2[0]: passive → enemy → dot atkPct 192, intervalSec 1, durationSec 99999, flavor sustained
skill2.note: 192% and 1s interval correct. Duration extended from 30s to 99999 (whole fight) because re-applied every reload (~5s cadence) so effectively permanent. 'First round only' mechanic abstracted away. noFb relic REMOVED (2026-07-15) — dot now correctly receives FB +50% timing. noRange kept (universal for sustained). charFixes.pullsPerSec=2.5 (video-confirmed 150rpm cadence) drives the reload-refresh economy.

skill2.kitLine: ATK ▲ 40.03% for 10 sec (on entering Full Burst)

burst.kitLine: Reload speed is fixed at a 99.96% increase for 10 sec
burst.note: Listed in both unmodeled and caveats. Reload economy is instead approximated via charFixes (pullsPerSec 2.5, reloadFrames 0). The 99.96% reload-speed buff during burst window is not separately modeled.
Ammo removal on burst cast not modeled. Partially offset by charFixes.reloadFrames=0 (rolling reload means no real ammo-gap penalty).
Forced reload mechanic not modeled. Same offset logic as ammo removal — rolling reload with reloadFrames 0 means the forced reload has negligible DPS impact in the sim.

Block present with correct value. ks-extracts finding F2 flags hit-rate as functionally unmodeled in the engine (measured core 0.20→0.90, x1.45 window) — the block may be engine-inert or partially inert depending on how the engine consumes hitRatePct.

burst.kitLine: Normal attacks deal True Damage for 10 sec
burst.note: Listed in unmodeled but NOT in caveats (asymmetric with the other three burst unmodeled lines). This is the F1 confound: without weaponSwap trueNormals, the engine cannot convert normal attacks to true damage. Interacts with skill1's trueDamagePct 34.99 — both are inert without the conversion mechanic.

documentedConfounds.text: F1: burst true-dmg conversion unmodeled → trueDamagePct 34.99 is engine-inert (needs weaponSwap trueNormals). Burst 'normal attacks deal True Damage for 10s' also unmodeled.
documentedConfounds.why: Both true-damage effects are inert, so they contribute zero — this makes the sim UNDER-count Jill's damage, pushing ratio > 1.0 (sim low). Consistent with HOT 1.042. However the note says per-hit popups matched at 99.7%, suggesting the true-damage window may be a small fraction of total DPS.

documentedConfounds.text: F2: Hit-Rate 80.78 block present but engine handling uncertain — measured core 0.20→0.90 (x1.45 window).
documentedConfounds.why: If hitRatePct is engine-inert, Jill loses a x1.45 core-hit multiplier during burst windows. This would make the sim UNDER-count, pushing ratio > 1.0. A live confound that could explain residual heat.

documentedConfounds.text: F4 cadence conflict RESOLVED: datamined 150 rpm = 2.5 shots/sec confirmed by focus video. charFixes.pullsPerSec=2.5 applied. Prior 1.67 heat was entirely cadence.
documentedConfounds.why: Resolved and video-confirmed. Per-hit popups matched at 99.7% across all four damage classes.

documentedConfounds.text: noFb relic REMOVED (2026-07-15): acid dot (192%/s sustained, permanent) no longer carries noFb:true. Now correctly receives FB +50% timing. Note predicts she grades ~1.07–1.34 hottest in high-FB comps.
documentedConfounds.why: Removing noFb increases sim damage for acid ticks inside FB windows. If the real game does NOT apply FB to sustained acid ticks (only Modernia Paradise Lost is type-exempt per DECISIONS), this over-counts. The note flags this as the primary residual risk and says to confirm x1.5 acid tick inside FB from video.

documentedConfounds.text: OPEN: confirm acid tick jumps x1.5 inside FB from battery-2-test-4 focus video (the 288,662 read was ambiguous on FB-state). Do NOT re-fudge.
documentedConfounds.why: If acid ticks do NOT get x1.5 in FB, the noFb removal over-counts. If they DO, the current model is correct. This is the single largest open measurement question.

documentedConfounds.text: Burst forced-reload economy unsupported: reload speed 99.96%, removes 100% ammo, forced reload — all unmodeled.
documentedConfounds.why: These affect reload timing/economy. With charFixes.reloadFrames=0 (rolling reload, fire never stops), the DPS impact of burst-window reload manipulation is minimal. Unlikely to move the needle.

authorIntentSummary: The override models Jill as a sustained-DPS AR attacker whose core loop is a permanent 192%/s acid dot (duration extended to whole-fight because reload-refresh makes it effectively permanent) plus a passive +30% normal attack buff, with burst providing +75% attack damage and +80.78% hit rate for 10s. The major calibration was cadence: video evidence proved her datamined 150rpm (2.5 shots/sec) rather than the AR class default, fixing a 1.67x heat entirely via charFixes.pullsPerSec. The noFb relic on her acid dot was removed (2026-07-15) so sustained ticks now correctly receive FB +50% timing, but this is the primary residual risk — the open question is whether acid ticks truly jump x1.5 inside FB. True-damage mechanics (skill1 trueDamagePct 34.99 and burst true-damage conversion) are blocked but engine-inert pending weaponSwap support.
